target_label: 2026-07 Metric Sherlock bug bounty contest (scope-needs-review mode)
paid_impact_focus: valid Sherlock High and Medium impacts for Metric; internal Critical maps to High; Low/QA/info are not promoted unless only used as triage notes
target_scopes: High: direct loss/theft/freezing of funds, severe oracle/pool/accounting failure, protocol insolvency, unauthorized value extraction, or accepted Sherlock High impact with realistic likelihood.

Medium: conditional but realistic fund loss, material pricing/accounting error, persistent DoS of core pool functions with accepted impact, or material invariant violation accepted as Sherlock Medium.
live_context_hint: Metric Sherlock contest source-page-verified: use filter=scope for exact files and filter=questions for trusted roles, token assumptions, oracle assumptions, invariants, design choices, severity definitions, and judging rules revision 8171fa8.
protocol_focus: Metric is an oracle-based OMM/AMM protocol across Ethereum, Base, and HyperEVM. Audit scoped Metric repositories for oracle anchored pricing, pool solvency, swap conservation, LP claims/fees, quote sanity, anchored band limits, no-trade-on-bad-oracle behavior, admin-boundary assumptions, and high/medium Sherlock-valid impacts.
attacker_profile: Unprivileged traders, LPs, pool creators where permissionless, arbitrageurs, griefers, and callers interacting with standard ERC20 pools. Trusted actors include Factory Owner, Pool Admin within documented bounds, Oracle ADMIN_ROLE, PriceProvider/AnchoredProvider AccessControl, and external protocol governance unless a genuine bypass exists.
core_invariants: Solvency: balances cover LP claims and fees; remove-liquidity works even while paused where specified.
Swap conservation: pool receives owed input; trader never receives more than the curve/oracle bounds allow.
Quote sanity: bid > 0 and bid < ask.
Anchored band: quote remains within mid ± (u + floor), including source mode.
No trade on bad oracle: stale price, Chainlink deviation, or L2 sequencer down reverts.
Scope mode is provisional: severity, duplicate, and build-repro blockers must be carried into every candidate.
high_value_surfaces: Oracle/price-provider math, stale/deviation/sequencer checks, anchored provider source-mode transitions.
Swap formula, rounding, drift, price-limit and bin index math.
Pool solvency, LP accounting, liquidity modify callbacks, protocol fees, fee limits, and treasury paths.
Factory/pool admin boundaries, arbitrary priceProvider changes, pause/configuration controls, and caps/timelocks.
Reentrancy around pool drain, callbacks, token transfers, and state updates.
Cross-component interactions among metric-core, metric-periphery, and smart-contracts-poc where scoped.
impact_mapping: Internal Critical -> Sherlock High: catastrophic drain, protocol-wide insolvency, or complete core pricing/settlement break.
High: direct or near-direct fund loss/freezing, severe accounting failure, or exploitable oracle/pool invariant break.
Medium: conditional but realistic loss, meaningful unfair distribution, persistent core-function DoS, or material invariant violation.
Reject gas, QA, informational, style-only, best-practice-only, and vague/unproved claims.
known_rejection_memory: Do not promote candidates requiring trusted actors to behave maliciously within documented authority unless proving a real unprivileged bypass or cap/timelock bypass.
Treat USDC/USDT token-specific nonstandard behavior as scope-needs-review unless Sherlock/Judge confirms explicitly named token exception.
Every candidate must carry duplicate-risk-needs-review until prior-art semantic cluster crosswalk is resolved.
Every PoC/build result must carry poc-env-needs-review if affected by unresolved dependency reproducibility.
Sherlock contest Questions page is now bound to judging rules revision 8171fa8; still carry duplicate-risk-needs-review and poc-env-needs-review until their separate Moon blockers clear.

Reject findings relying only on malicious Factory Owner, Oracle ADMIN_ROLE, trusted integrated protocol governance, or unbounded Pool Admin behavior unless proving the specific cap/timelock bypass allowed by contest questions.
Reject non-standard ERC20 pool-created issues; USDC and USDT are explicitly in scope.
Do not claim off-chain oracle update misbehavior/staleness as a bug unless the in-scope code violates its stated stale/deviation/sequencer/band guards.
manual_deepwiki_verification_gate: Open all generated DeepWiki links in Brave and wait for Null verification before continuing post-setup workflows.

contest_questions_facts.tokens: Standard ERC-20 only. USDC and USDT in scope. Non-standard ERC20 pool-created issues out of scope.
trusted_roles: Factory Owner trusted: fee defaults/caps, poolDeployer once, protocol pause, treasury sweeps.
Pool Admin semi-trusted and bounded: valid only if caps/timelocks bypassed and Medium+.
PriceProvider/AnchoredProvider factory trusted but provider params bounded.
contest_questions_facts.oracle_assumption: Off-chain oracle price updates are considered correct and not stale; compressed oracle updates considered correct/not stale unless code guard issue.
invariants: Solvency: balances cover LP claims + owed fees; remove liquidity works while paused.
Swap conservation: exact settlement / IncorrectDelta otherwise; no value creation/leak.
Quote sanity: bid > 0 and bid < ask.
Anchored band: quote including source mode stays within mid ± (u + floor).
No trade on bad oracle: stale price, Chainlink deviation, L2 sequencer down revert.
design_choices: Pure oracle-anchored pricing; no internal price discovery, no DEX cross-check except Chainlink deviation.
Rounding always favors pool; if not and Medium+ impact, valid.
Anchored band clipping is safety boundary; incorrect band math with Medium+ impact can be valid.

severity_defs.critical: Direct loss without external limitations, exploitable any moment, >20% and >100 USD; excludes yield/rewards.
severity_defs.high: Direct loss without extensive external limitations >1% and >10 USD, or to-be-claimed yield/rewards >20% and >100 USD.
severity_defs.medium: Loss under conditions/constrained >0.01% and >10 USD, yield/rewards >1% and >10 USD, or core functionality break causing qualifying loss.
